title: `IntersectionObserver` observes boxless `display:contents` wrapper, breaking visibility detection for dynamic outputs
rationale: Facts: Reporter gadenbuie (MEMBER) reports that a dynamically-rendered output (uiOutput()/htmlOutput()) inside a boxless `display:contents` wrapper never fires its IntersectionObserver, so the server keeps it suspended and it renders nothing; most visibly hit via bslib popover()/tooltip() content with no title (cross-linked to rstudio/bslib#1326). Includes a minimal reprex and root-cause analysis pointing at `resolveObservableTarget()` in srcts/src/shiny/index.ts. Classification hypothesis: regression in shiny's observer-resolution logic introduced by a prior PR. Duplicate check: no other open shiny issue covers this; it is the upstream tracking issue opened in response to the downstream bslib report (bslib#1326), not a duplicate of it (different repo, this is the root-cause issue). Wrong-location check: correctly filed in shiny — root cause is shiny's srcts observer code, not bslib. Regression check: reporter confirms via `git bisect` that behavior differs materially between shiny 1.13.0 (works) and 1.14.0 (broken), bisected to PR #4388 — meets the evidence bar. Reproduction plan: run the provided minimal reprex (bslib popover() with a title-less uiOutput()) in shiny 1.14.0 vs 1.13.0; open the popover and observe the body stays empty only on 1.14.0+. Impact: affects any dynamically-rendered output revealed by relocating a node into a boxless display:contents container without an accompanying box-size change (popovers/tooltips without titles is the known trigger); a downstream mitigation already shipped in bslib (rstudio/bslib#1327) which narrows current production exposure, but the underlying shiny defect is unresolved and the team's own RFC (in-thread, MEMBER/COLLABORATOR discussion) is actively weighing a long-term architectural fix (autonomous `<shiny-html-output>` custom element). Priority: High — confirmed high-confidence regression via bisect, affecting a common UI pattern, with active core-team engineering discussion; not Critical because a workaround is already deployed downstream in bslib. Recommended next action: track engineering decision between the discussed options (global min-width CSS rule as the shipped short-term mitigation vs. the longer-term custom-element migration); no further AI action needed beyond labeling given the issue is already under active human discussion by MEMBER/COLLABORATOR participants.

title: Fails to render dynamic contents of popover if it does not have a title.
rationale: Facts: Reporter averissimo provides a full reprex (bslib popover/tooltip wrapping a uiOutput with no title, dynamic content never renders) plus session info showing shiny 1.14.0. MEMBER gadenbuie reproduced it, bisected the regression to rstudio/shiny#4388, and opened the upstream root-cause issue rstudio/shiny#4406. Classification hypothesis: regression, downstream symptom of a shiny-side defect. Duplicate check: not a duplicate of shiny#4406 (different repository; this is the original user report that led to the upstream tracking issue, and bslib is shipping its own temporary fix here rather than just closing/redirecting). Wrong-location check: root cause lives in shiny, but the team explicitly stated they will land a small temporary fix in bslib (a min-width workaround) pending the upstream shiny fix, so this issue legitimately stays open in bslib rather than being purely 'wrong location'. Regression check: confirmed — reporter states 'only happens since shiny@1.14.0' and MEMBER reproduced the same with a side-by-side 1.13.0-vs-1.14.0 screenshot comparison. Reproduction plan: use the module-based reprex in the second comment (POPOVER_TITLE toggle) with shiny >= 1.14.0. Impact: any bslib popover()/tooltip() without a title that wraps dynamically-rendered output silently fails to render its content — a workaround (min-width style or adding a title) is documented in-thread and a temporary bslib-side fix is already planned. Priority: Medium — real regression with user-facing breakage, but a known workaround exists and a fix is already in progress, so it is not the highest-severity open item. Recommended next action: land the promised temporary min-width mitigation in bslib and track resolution against the upstream fix in rstudio/shiny#4406.

title: logging.disable(logging.INFO) conflicts with Shiny's logging and also prevents it from launching browser on start ("--launch-browser")
rationale: Facts: Original 2023 report from Dene33 shows that disabling the root logger's INFO level (or, per a 2026-06-28 follow-up comment from a different user, running with `--log-level=warning`) prevents `--launch-browser` from firing. A py-shiny COLLABORATOR (jcheng5) confirmed in 2023 that shiny uses log interception on the child uvicorn process to detect readiness, calling the approach 'weird and fragile,' and floated a fallback strategy (poll for a socket connection) but no fix has shipped. The most recent comment includes reporter-labeled 'AI take' analysis pointing at `_main.py`/`_launchbrowser.py`/`_autoreload.py` and proposing to raise the readiness log message to CRITICAL — this is untrusted, unverified third-party analysis and is treated as a lead, not a fact. Classification hypothesis: bug — a known architectural fragility (log-level-gated readiness detection) with a newly reported second trigger. Duplicate check: searched py-shiny issues for 'launch-browser'/'log-level' combinations; found none. Wrong-location check: py-shiny is correct — the affected code (`_launchbrowser.py`, CLI log-level handling) lives here. Regression check: not a regression — this has been reproducible since at least 2023 and is a longstanding design limitation, not a behavior change from a prior release. Reproduction plan: run `shiny run` with `--log-level=warning` (or call `logging.disable(logging.INFO)` before app startup) and confirm the browser does not auto-launch despite `--launch-browser`. Impact: affects any user who tunes logging verbosity (a common production/ops practice) and relies on `--launch-browser`; workaround is to avoid raising the log level or to launch the browser manually. Priority: Medium — valid, reproducible bug with moderate impact and a viable workaround; root cause already diagnosed by a collaborator, so it is well-defined enough to act on without further clarification. Recommended next action: implement a level-independent readiness signal (e.g., the socket-polling fallback jcheng5 proposed in 2023, or a dedicated internal log record that bypasses level filtering) rather than relying on the INFO-level uvicorn message.

title: Update groq and Gemini default models
rationale: Facts: Reporter thisisnic notes that Groq is deprecating `llama-3.1-8b-instant` in August (per Groq's published deprecation docs) with `openai/gpt-oss-20b` as the recommended replacement, and that chatlas's default Google Gemini model (currently 2.5) has fallen behind ellmer's, which recently updated its default to `gemini-3.5-flash` (tidyverse/ellmer#1026). No comments yet. Classification hypothesis: enhancement/maintenance — updating default model constants ahead of an upstream provider deprecation and to stay in parity with the sibling R package ellmer. Duplicate check: no other open chatlas issue requests this; not a duplicate. Wrong-location check: chatlas is correct — the default model constants for `ChatGroq()`/`ChatGoogle()` live in this package. Regression check: not applicable — this is a forward-looking maintenance request, not a behavior regression. Reproduction plan: not applicable; verify by checking `ChatGroq()`/`ChatGoogle()` default model arguments against Groq's deprecation schedule and ellmer's current default. Impact: if left unaddressed past Groq's August deprecation date, any chatlas user relying on the default Groq model will hit a removed/deprecated model; the Gemini default lagging ellmer is a lower-urgency parity gap. Priority: Medium — well-defined, low-effort change with a concrete external deadline (Groq's August deprecation) that could otherwise cause user-facing breakage if missed. Recommended next action: update the default model constants for `ChatGroq()` and `ChatGoogle()` before Groq's August cutover; coordinate with ellmer to keep defaults in sync going forward.
